naive_cost: Treat timer enablement as sufficient and wait for an unobserved research job to expose queue, checkpoint, completion-event, and review failures.
opportunities_considered: symmetry reduction, canonical augmentation, and orbit quotients
bitsets, batching, vectorization, and incremental evaluation
memoization, caching, reusable clauses, and reusable proof prefixes
decomposition, cube-and-conquer, meet-in-the-middle, and resumable segments
compressed artifacts and streamed or hash-verified manifests
sound cheap prefilters and independently checked coverage
chosen_reductions: Use the repository's 41-block control witness and direct exact checker as a tiny deterministic payload, split into two identical bounded segments so the first checkpoint requires a continuation review and the second creates a final completion event.
expected_throughput_gain: The full lifecycle is exercised in seconds with negligible artifact growth, instead of consuming a real SAT tranche merely to test orchestration.
soundness_basis: Each segment independently enumerates all 495 four-subsets through checkers/verify_cover.py, requires exactly 41 distinct six-blocks, and records correctness and decision-value gates in durable JSON.
remains_uncompressed: This canary verifies orchestration and the known 41-block positive control only. It does not test solver proof production, close any 40-block frontier node, or support an exact-value claim.
